author: peterchen-intel
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' when initializing GhApi client

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_ubuntu_22_arm64_cross_compilation
failed_jobs.symptom: Overall status check exits 1 because Smart_CI failed

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
                                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: fastcore 2.0.1 (released ~2026-07-08) changed the behavior of the L class so that L(funcs) no longer returns a fastcore.L instance with the starmap method; instead it falls back to a plain Python list. The ghapi 1.0.4 library's GhApi.__init__ calls L(funcs).starmap(...) and therefore raises AttributeError. The Smart CI step uses ghapi to enumerate PR labels/components and fails immediately on initialization. This is a transitive dependency version bump that broke an implicit API contract between fastcore and ghapi. This is the 5th occurrence of the same failure today.
reproduction_steps: N/A - requires CI environment with fastcore>=2.0.1 installed. Locally: pip install 'fastcore>=2.0.1' ghapi==1.0.4 && python3 -c "from ghapi.all import GhApi; GhApi()"
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore to <2.0.1 in the Smart CI requirements: fastcore<2.0.1 or fastcore==1.8.4
Or upgrade ghapi to a version that is compatible with fastcore 2.0.1
Update .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt (or similar) with the pinned version
Merge the fix PR quickly - this is breaking ALL merge-queue runs today
prevention_strategies: Pin all transitive dependencies in CI scripts to exact versions (e.g., requirements.txt with ==). Add a CI health-check job that runs Smart CI initialization in isolation so version conflicts are caught before merge.
historical_context: This is occurrence #5 of this exact failure pattern today (2026-07-09). First seen at 07:11 UTC. Prior occurrences affected PRs #36780, #36773, #36504, and #36781 (earlier run 29002123148). Pattern hash: 7862e85dd17a5df8.
